methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 16 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
AbCellera is a public-stage biotech and health company operating in a sector with 3 identified public comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers, including Tempus AI, Abridge, Ultima Genomics, Eikon Therapeutics, and Freenome. The peer universe remains fragmented across valuation tiers and business models.

Peer-median triangulation is not applicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow peer set; while 16 same-sector peers exist, fewer than three datapoints satisfy narrow stage and valuation-tier filters, making sector-wide aggregation misleading. Public-comp anchoring cannot be executed because AbCellera's revenue is not on file—a typical constraint for private company valuation. The three public biotech peers trade at a median P/S multiple of 7.4x; this figure cannot be applied without target revenue disclosure.

The analysis cannot support a triangulated valuation range given the absence of applicable methodologies. Key uncertainties include AbCellera's current revenue run-rate, margin trajectory, and whether its technology platform warrants multiples above or below the 7.4x public-sector median. Additional disclosure of financial performance or a narrower cohort of directly comparable clinical-stage or platform-biotech peers would be required to anchor a defensible valuation. Reliance on private fundraising rounds or transaction comps would be the next logical approach.
